Plugin.Name: msf
Plugin.Overview: A free and open-source, user-facing all-in-one management tool for DNS and proxy (mihomo / sing-box, in development), packaged for Unraid. msf installs a lightweight Settings control page plus a self-managed local service. The Settings page is only for enabling the rc service, setting the listen host/port, choosing the data directory, viewing status, and opening the separate msf WebUI. The full DNS/proxy management interface runs in that WebUI. After you complete the in-app setup wizard, msf can run MosDNS (DNS), Mihomo (proxy/TProxy), and the required nftables rules. Online MosDNS/Mihomo/Zashboard core downloads are verified against GitHub Release API asset SHA-256 digests before install; local uploads remain user-supplied files and are not marked as project-verified. Default sample subscriptions/nodes are inert placeholders only. Persistent data lives under /mnt/user/appdata/msf. Update and remove msf from the Unraid Plugins page (not via the in-app updater).
Plugin.Changes: ### msf 0.3.3 - Added the experimental Cloudflare Redirect CLI plugin: `msf cloudflare-redirect` / `msf cf-redirect`. - Added start, stop, scan, apply, and status actions for Cloudflare CDN IPv4/IPv6 scanning, MosDNS direct-client injection, and rollback. - Added the default `configs/cloudflare-redirect/cfyouxuan.yaml` file. The plugin remains disabled by default and can be enabled after editing manual domains or subscriptions. - Added setup preflight checks for root permission, timezone, port 53, and runtime port occupancy, with automatic systemd-resolved DNS stub remediation when supported. - Improved component update verification state by preserving installed digest, verification source, and verification time, with clearer local-upload provenance. - Improved MosDNS rule-source dialogs, adblock/online-routing source interactions, and rewrite category mapping. - Fixed self-update installation under systemd so the installer is detached from the current service lifecycle. - Persisted sidebar group open state within the session and expanded README documentation for Cloudflare Redirect usage and troubleshooting.
